creator: mi+kde@aldan.algebra.com
text:
On Monday 26 March 2007 01:26, Thierry Thomas wrote:
 = The tests are not failing, and I don't propose to disable them, but just
 = to move them to the regression-test target (which is automatically
 = launched by pointyhat or tinderbox).
 
 The error log:
 
 http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/amd64-errorlogs/e.5.2007032316/tclX-8.4.log
 
 shows some failures in the nice.test, which are fatal in the current version 
 of the port.
 
 = The failure is when the port rebuilds man pages, and it is addressed by
 = patch-af.
 
 Khmm, this I was not aware off. Nor do I see it in my builds. Your patch may, 
 indeed, fix that, but I'd like to figure out, what's wrong with the nice 
 command now.
 
 It was all working fine for months (years?) until something changed in the 
 jail-implementation...
 
 	-mi
